summary: Excellent audience recording captures a top‑notch early‑'80s show highlighted by a fiery Bertha opener, scorching Mexicali Blues and soaring Eyes of the World.
review: Both reviewers rave about the performance – Jerry’s warm‑up on Bertha leads into hot picking on Mexicali Blues, a powerful sequence of Althea > Lost Lady > Big River Blues > Let It Grow, and an inspired Eyes of the World that locks with Brent’s organ. The audience source is praised as crystal‑clear, though Phil’s bass sits a bit low in the mix. Overall the show flows brilliantly from first set climax into a second‑set jam marathon.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviews are highly positive (5 and 4 stars) and emphasize strong musical moments with only minor mix quibbles, yielding a rating just above the average of the star scores.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and well‑balanced audience recording; mix is generally right but Phil’s bass is low in the mix throughout the tour.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set builds smoothly from a strong opener through an energetic first half, then launches into a jam‑heavy second set with seamless transitions and memorable climaxes.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Warmed up on Bertha, delivered hot picking on Mexicali Blues, drove the jams with fast strumming and dazzling leads, especially on Eyes of the World.
band_member_comments.Phil: Excellent playing but buried low in the mix; his Earthquake Space is highlighted as a standout moment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Kept mostly in the shadows in the mix, contribution felt more than heard.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent adds church‑organ texture on Cold Rain & Snow and locks in with Jerry during Eyes of the World.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill played a mean cymbal; drummers were ready for launch and provided solid support throughout.
